task_completion.description: The map application is opened and displays Kabul, Afghanistan to establish the starting landmark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view shows the city of Kabul, Afghanistan. The search bar may contain 'Kabul' as permitted by the task.

task_completion.description: The map is manually panned northwards from Kabul along the highway.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Sequential screenshots show the map view shifting north from Kabul along the main highway, indicating manual navigation rather than an instant search jump.

task_completion.description: The city of Charikar is successfully located and visible on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map displays the city or label for 'Charikar', located north of Kabul.

task_completion.description: A location pin or selection marker is placed on Charikar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropped pin, waypoint marker, or active selection highlight is visible exactly on the city of Charikar.

task_completion.description: Charikar is set as the navigation destination.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A directions or routing panel is active, and Charikar (or the specific dropped pin at Charikar) is populated in the 'Destination' or 'To' field.

integrity.description: The agent must not use the search function to find Charikar.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify the search bar across all screenshots. The text 'Charikar' must NEVER appear in the map's search input field.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a real map application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface belongs to a genuine mapping service (e.g., Google Maps, Apple Maps, OpenStreetMap) with standard map controls, not a static image or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to navigate.
integrity.visual_evidence: Actions like panning, clicking, and setting the destination are done via the UI (mouse clicks/drags visible or implied by UI state changes), without using terminal commands or scripts to bypass the interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Charikar is a real city in Afghanistan, serving as the capital of Parwan Province. It is located approximately 54-62 km (roughly 60km) directly north of Kabul. The correct English spelling is 'Charikar'. The VLM must strictly ensure that 'Charikar' is never typed into the search bar, as the task requires manual panning north from Kabul to locate it.
